title: Eurycaulis cumulatus
taxonomy: Eurycaulis cumulatus is a species of coniferous tree in the family Taxaceae. It is native to China and is also known as the 'Cumulatus Yew'. The classification of Eurycaulis cumulatus is as follows: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, the phylum Pinophyta, the class Pinopsida, the order Pinales, and the family Taxaceae. This species is closely related to the genus Taxus, which includes the English Yew. Eurycaulis cumulatus is considered an important and unique species due to its distinct characteristics and habitat.

preparations: Eurycaulis cumulatus, commonly known as the Tibetan rhododendron, is used in traditional medicine. The leaves and flowers of Eurycaulis cumulatus are used to make teas and infusions that are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties.
